targets.content: To promote the share of public transport in motorized travel in big-andmedium-sized cities reaching 30% by 2020

To achieve the peaking of carbon dioxide emissions around 2030 and making best efforts to peak early

To lower carbon dioxide emissions per unit of GDP by 60% to 65% from the 2005 level by 2030
aims to have CO2 emissions peak before 2030 and achieve carbon neutrality before 2060; to lower CO2 emissions per unit of GDP by over 65% from the 2005 level,
By 2030, the proportion of new energy and clean energy-powered vehicles will reach about 40% of all the vehicles sold in that year,

(By 2030) the carbon emission intensity of converted turnover of commercial vehicles will decrease about 9.5% from 2020 level, and the comprehensive energy consumption per unit of converted turnover of railways will drop by 10% from 2020 level,
(By 2030) oil consumption by land transportation strives to peak
The new round of NDC targets proposed by China aims to reduce net greenhouse gas emissions across the entire economy by 7%–10% from peak levels by 2035, striving to achieve even better results.

The proportion of non-fossil energy consumption in total energy consumption will reach over 30%; the total installed capacity of wind and solar power will reach more than six times that of 2020, striving to reach 3.6 billion kilowatts;
By 2035, civil aviation transportation will strive to achieve carbon-neutral growth

Hong Kong: The goal is to achieve zero carbon emissions in the transport sector by 2050. (…) The Hong Kong Airport Authority, together with major aviation-related business partners, has committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions by the end of 2050

Hong Kong: The "Hong Kong Electric Vehicle Adoption Roadmap" proposes to stop registering new gasoline and hybrid private cars by 2035 or earlier. (…) the goal is to increase the number of public and private charging parking spaces to approximately 200,000 by mid-2027.
Macao: "The goal of achieving 100% zero-emission vehicles (electric vehicles or other technologies) in newly registered light-duty cars and motorcycles means that by 2035, the electric penetration rate of newly registered light-duty cars and motorcycles will reach 100%."

measures.instrument: General transport and land use planning
measures.quote: Integrate low-carbon development in the entire process of urban planning
Promote the share of public transport in motorized travel in large- and medium-sized cities (targeting 30% mode share by 2020);
accelerate the construction of a comprehensive transportation network,
channel energy into multi-modal transportation,

measures.instrument: Freight transport shifting to rail or inland waterways
measures.quote: and increase the share of railways and waterways in the integrated  transportation
China will optimize passenger transport, and guide the scale-up and intensive operation of those passenger transport enterprises
Green logistics will be developed speedily, transport resources will be optimized and the overall utilization efficiency will be advanced.
Measures will be taken for expediting vehicles and vessels that are powered by new energy and clean energy,
popularizing intelligent transportation,
pushing forward the electrification of railways,
giving impetus to constructing hydrogen filling stations,

measures.instrument: Support on-shore power and electric charging facilities in ports
measures.quote: and normalizing the utilization of shore-end cable for vessels at ports.
China will move faster to put in place a convenient, efficient and moderately advanced  network system for battery charging and swap.
Energy efficiency standards for fuel-powered vehicles and vessels will be upgraded,
energy efficiency labeling system for transport equipment will be improved,

measures.instrument: Vehicle restrictions (import, age, access, sale, taxation)
measures.quote: and timeworn vehicles and vessels that are highly energy-consuming and of high emissions will be phased out.

China will further actively guide low-carbon travel. China will quicken the construction of large-capacity public transport infrastructure including urban railways, special traffic lanes and bus rapid transit systems,
China will quicken the construction of large-capacity public transport infrastructure including urban railways, special traffic lanes and bus rapid transit systems,
and strengthen the construction of urban slow traffic systems, namely, special bicycle lanes, pedestrian walkways, etc.
By making comprehensive use of legal, economic, administrative and other management measures, China will put particular emphasis on combating urban traffic congestion.

measures.instrument: General transport and land use planning
measures.quote: Build a comprehensive three-dimensional transportation network, integrate the concept of green development throughout the entire process of transportation infrastructure planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance, and actively promote the construction of green railways, green highways, green waterways, green ports, green airports, and green hubs.

measures.instrument: Freight transport shifting to rail or inland waterways
measures.quote: Accelerate the optimization and adjustment of the transportation structure, continue to deepen the construction of special railways and special railway lines, actively develop direct river-sea and river-sea combined transportation, strive to increase the proportion of combined iron and water transportation, accelerate the promotion of the "revolution to rail" and "revolution to water" of bulk cargo in coastal ports,
vigorously develop multi-modal high-efficiency transportation organization modes such as combined transportation, drop-and-pull transportation and joint distribution,
improve the efficiency of transportation operations, promote the standardization of freight vehicles, and accelerate the application of recyclable standardized logistics turnover boxes.
Promote the development of smart transportation, and actively develop new technologies, new business formats,
new models such as autonomous driving and shared cars.
new models such as autonomous driving and shared cars.
Accelerate the development, promotion and application of key energy-saving and low-carbon technologies and products in the transportation sector.
Continue to increase the retention rate of new energy vehicles.

Promote the realization of electrification, new energy and cleanness of urban public transportation and urban logistics and distribution vehicles and promote the use of new energy for private cars and trucks.
Promote the application of low-carbon energy ships
explore the application of biomass fuels and other synthetic fuels in civil aviation.
explore the application of biomass fuels and other synthetic fuels in civil aviation.
Increase the application of renewable energy such as solar energy, wind energy and geothermal energy in transportation infrastructure
accelerate the improvement of the LNG and hydrogen energy supply (filling) system,
accelerate the improvement of the LNG and hydrogen energy supply (filling) system,
focus on solving the problems of difficult approval, construction, and operation of marine LNG refueling stations.
Build (near) zero-carbon hubs, stations and ports (port areas). Speed up the construction of a green travel system.
Strengthen the comprehensive management of urban traffic congestion,
give priority to the development of urban public transportation,
encourage the public to preferentially choose urban public transportation to travel,
improve the urban slow traffic system, and increase the proportion of green travel.
Actively develop large-capacity and high-efficiency inter-regional rapid passenger transport services with high-speed rail and aviation as the mainstay,
improve the level of rail transit commuting in urban agglomerations,
encourage the mass transit form operation of intercity road transportation in areas with high passenger demand.

measures.instrument: Support on-shore power and electric charging facilities in ports
measures.quote: shore power,
aircraft auxiliary power unit replacement, and airport photovoltaic systems.
promote clean energy development in shipping and aviation.
By 2035, civil aviation transportation will strive to achieve carbon-neutral growth,

The Hong Kong Airport Authority, together with major aviation-related business partners, has committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions by the end of 2050 and has formulated a carbon management action plan, including the electrification of vehicles in airport restricted areas, the increase of electric vehicle charging stations, and the trial use of hydrogen energy.

To promote the development of new energy industries in the aviation sector, the Hong Kong SAR Government will consult with the Mainland Government to lead a major global supplier of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) to develop in the Greater Bay Area, in line with the 2030 target of achieving 1% to 2% SAF usage for flights departing from Hong Kong International Airport.

As the first port in Asia to mandate the switching of ocean-going vessels to low-sulfur fuel, Hong Kong is working with the Mainland to reduce ship pollutant emissions, including jointly implementing a ship emission control area in the Pearl River Delta waters with the Guangdong Provincial Government. The "Green Marine Fuel Bunkering Action Plan" was announced in 2024, aiming to develop Hong Kong into a high-quality green marine fuel bunkering center.

measures.instrument: General transport and land use planning
measures.quote: Coordinate planning to guide the green and low-carbon transformation of urban and rural construction. Advocate green and low-carbon planning and design concepts, combine with the implementation of land spatial planning, optimize urban and rural structures and spatial layouts, protect natural landscapes, enhance the spatial safety and resilience of towns, and carry out the construction of green and low-carbon communities.
Optimize the transportation structure,

measures.instrument: Freight transport shifting to rail or inland waterways
measures.quote: vigorously promote the high-quality development of long-distance transportation of bulk goods and containers from road to water and from road to rail,
as well as multimodal transport,
promote clean transportation in key industries and ports, and create a leading area for clean transportation.
Promote the transformation of existing transportation infrastructure,
improve the greening and intelligentization of newly built, renovated (expanded) stations, airports, and port facilities,
improve the greening and intelligentization of newly built, renovated (expanded) stations, airports, and port facilities,
and improve the infrastructure network including charging (swapping) stations,
hydrogen refueling stations,
Vigorously promote new energy vehicles, drive the electric replacement of urban public service vehicles,
a modern comprehensive transportation system will be basically established, and new energy vehicles will become the mainstream of newly sold vehicles.

Actively develop green trade. Promote the green transformation of goods trade, launch a green trade public service platform, compile and distribute the "Handbook on Green and Low-Carbon Development for Foreign Trade Enterprises," hold specialized training sessions, and select best practice cases in green trade.

Support free trade zones in taking the lead in promoting carbon peaking and carbon neutrality, and support qualified free trade zones and free trade ports in establishing voluntary mechanisms to improve environmental performance.
Launch a nationwide green and low-carbon campaign, guide the public to choose green travel options,

The Hong Kong SAR Government's inter-departmental working group on hydrogen energy is promoting the local application of hydrogen energy through pilot projects. As of October 2025, applications for 28 hydrogen energy pilot projects have been approved in principle, including hydrogen buses, hydrogen street washing vehicles, and the first public hydrogen refueling station. In mid-2024, the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) Government announced the "Hong Kong Hydrogen Energy Development Strategy," proposing four major strategies: "improving regulations," "establishing standards," "aligning with the market," and "prudently advancing." This aims to create a favorable environment for local hydrogen energy development in a stable and orderly manner, while also expanding cooperation between Hong Kong and the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area, and even globally, integrating Hong Kong into the overall national development strategy and developing new productive forces.
electrification of vehicles in airport restricted areas,
the increase of electric vehicle charging stations,
and the trial use of hydrogen energy

Macao: Formulate the "Macao Electric Vehicle Promotion Plan," study the year for ceasing new registrations of various types of fuel vehicles, and launch the "Subsidy Plan for Eliminating Old Motorcycles and Replacing Them with New Electric Motorcycles."

Social awareness of green and low-carbon development has significantly increased. Utilized national low-carbon day and other themed activities to popularize climate change knowledge and publicize achievements, continuously carrying out Green Travel Promotion Month and Public Transportation Promotion Week activities.
